title: Domain IR
description: A business domain groups one or more Integration artifacts, declares canonical domain events, and sets domain-wide policy defaults that individual integrations inherit.

name.description: Kebab-case domain identifier. Must match metadata.domain in every registered Integration.

owner.description: Team or individual responsible for this domain.

classification.description: Default data classification applied to all integrations in this domain. Individual integrations may declare a stricter value; never a looser one.

integrations.description: Ordered list of Integration artifacts that belong to this domain. Order implies nothing about deployment sequence.

path.description: Path to the integration-ir.yaml, relative to the domain.yaml file.

layer.description: Architectural layer this integration belongs to. Individual flows may override via flows[].layer when a single deployed unit spans layers.

events.description: Canonical domain events — messages that cross integration boundaries within this domain. The Domain owns these schemas; integrations reference them as dependencies.

schemaRef.description: Path to the canonical schema file, relative to the domain.yaml. All integrations that produce or consume this event must reference this schema.

version.description: Semver of this event contract. Bump on breaking changes.

compatibility.description: Schema evolution policy: backward = new readers can read old data; forward = old readers can read new data; full = both; none = breaking changes allowed.

producers.description: Integration names (metadata.name) that emit this event. Informational; ir-validator cross-checks.

consumers.description: Integration names (metadata.name) that consume this event. Informational; ir-validator cross-checks.

policy.description: Domain-wide defaults inherited by all integrations. Integration-level declarations override these; they never loosen them.

auth.description: Default authentication scheme for channels in this domain.

channel.description: Logical name of the domain-level DLQ channel. Each integration is expected to declare a matching channel.

dataClassification.description: Minimum data classification enforced across all channels and messages in this domain. Integrations may declare a stricter value.

pii.description: When true, all integrations in this domain are assumed to carry PII unless a message explicitly declares pii: false. Drives pii-flow-checker behaviour.

nonFunctionals.description: Domain-level NFR defaults. Integrations may declare stricter values.
